name: == vs equals() on Java Strings
question_text:
Consider:

String a = new String("hi");
String b = new String("hi");
boolean r1 = (a == b);
boolean r2 = a.equals(b);

What are the values of r1 and r2?
general_feedback: == compares object references, not contents. Because the two new String calls create distinct objects, a == b is false. equals on String compares the character sequences, which are equal, so it returns true.
correct_feedback: Correct — == compares references (different objects), equals compares content (same characters).

answers.feedback: == compares references (different objects), equals compares content (same characters).
== is true only when both sides reference the exact same object; new creates distinct objects.
The character contents match, so equals returns true.
Both outcomes are reversed; == is false and equals is true here.
